for.p: A person (a “service-provider”) concerned with the provision of a service to the public or a section of the public (for payment or not)

to.p: discriminate against a person requiring the service by not providing the person with the service

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
(a) as to the terms on which A provides the service to B;
(b) by terminating the provision of the service to B;
(c) by subjecting B to any other detriment.

condition.p: providing the service
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: in relation to the provision of the service, harass—

ul.li: (a) a person requiring the service,
or (b) a person to whom the service-provider provides the service

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: victimise a person requiring the service by not providing the person with the service

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
(a) as to the terms on which A provides the service to B;
(b) by terminating the provision of the service to B;
(c) by subjecting B to any other detriment.

condition.p: providing the service
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

to.p: do anything that constitutes discrimination, harassment or victimisation

condition.p: exercising a public function that is not the provision of a service to the public or a section of the public
this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule

for.p: a person who exercises a public function that is not the provision of a service to the public or a section of the public

condition.p: this applies to the protected characteristic of—

ul.li: (a) age,
so far as relating to persons who have not attained the age of 18;
(b) marriage and civil partnership

condition.p: this applies to discrimination, harassment or victimisation—

ul.li: (a) that is prohibited by Part 4 (premises),
5 (work) or 6 (education),
or (b) that would be so prohibited but for an express exception

condition.p: this applies to —

ul.li: (a) a breach of an equality clause or rule;
(b) anything that would be a breach of an equality clause or rule but for section 69 or Part 2 of Schedule 7;
(c) a breach of a non-discrimination rule
